channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: OPM inspector general investigating DOGE emails, access to agency data
item.description: The inspector general for the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) told lawmakers he is investigating the use of a new data system at the agency that has been part of a broader effort by Elon Musk and his allies to email federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Vance ramps up his clout in Trump world
item.description: Vice President Vance’s clout is on the rise, taking on the role of pot stirrer at the White House and on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Fetterman: 'Columbia pays for its failure' with $400M in grants axed
item.description: The campus was a hot spot for pro-Palestine protests after the Hamas attack on Oct. 7, 2023, sparked discourse in the region.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP seeks spending cuts, defense increases to avert shutdown
item.description: The bill's rollout kicks off a crucial stretch for GOP leadership to lock down support ahead of Friday's shutdown deadline with a Trump-backed strategy that has drawn the ire of Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Unions ask court to stop DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A group of labor unions are asking a federal court for an emergency order to stop Elon Musk 's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing the sensitive Social Security data of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ras Baraka has highest favorability among Democratic candidates for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New polling from Fairleigh Dickinson University gave us some early insight on who voters are looking into in the race for New Jersey governor. Democratic voters weighed in on the candidates' favorability.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Renewed debate sparks discussion on 'My Safe Florida Home' program
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis wants more Floridians to benefit from the "My Safe Florida Home" program, which provides storm-hardening home improvements for homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota among states that sued Trump administration over federal workers' mass firings
item.description: The lawsuit says the mass firings will cause irreparable burdens and expenses on the states, because they will have to support recently unemployed workers and adjudicate claims of unemployment assistance.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration cancels $400M in grants and contracts with Columbia University
item.description: Multiple federal agencies announced Friday that they're canceling grants and contracts because of what the government describes as the Ivy League school’s lackluster efforts to squelch antisemitism on campus. The U of M is one of five colleges under new federal antisemitism investigations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: A downbeat Minnesota budget forecast initiates lawmaker blame game
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers received an economic budget forecast that shows less wiggle room as they write a new budget and an even larger looming deficit in the future. Gov. Tim Walz and DFL leaders blamed President Donald Trump while Republican leaders said Democrats need to look in the mirror.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Las Vegas homeless camps just move when outreach comes, but proposal might change that
item.description: When homeless camps get out of control, local governments send teams to clean up. That can be a disaster, costing people what few possessions they have. But trash and unsanitary conditions are becoming a bigger priority as residents go to elected officials demanding action.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Following Trump’s lead, Gov. Abbott pushes state agencies to end telework
item.description: The directive comes as some state agencies have downsized their office spaces after the pandemic forced many employees to work remotely.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Pooja Salhotra, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida House speaker responds to Democrats call to investigate insurance companies
item.description: Democrats at the Florida State House are calling on Gov. Ron DeSantis and the House Speaker to further investigate Florida's insurance companies.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump passes Clinton, gives longest modern address to joint session of Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump gave the longest address to a joint session of Congress in modern history on Tuesday night, surpassing former President Bill Clinton’s previous mark.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: More states want to stop taxing groceries
item.description: The number of states imposing sales taxes on groceries has shrunk over the years, and the number may decrease further in the coming months
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Proposal would use cameras to ticket double parking in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- A proposed pilot program aims to crack down on various curb violations, including drivers who double park in New York City. The program would use cameras to issue automatic tickets.
item.dc:creator: Kiran Dhillon, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada 'a perfect storm for homicides' in domestic violence cases, lawmakers told
item.description: FBI statistics show Nevada is "a perfect storm for homicides" and the state ranks third for men murdering women, the leader of SafeNest said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs if Canada retaliates
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday threatened to add additional taxes on Canada if the U.S.'s northern neighbor Canada retaliates with its own after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau slammed Trump’s newly implemented tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: The White House on Tuesday unveiled the list of guests who will sit with first lady Melania Trump at the Capitol during President Donald Trump’s address to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street falls again as losses wipe out all post-election gains for the S&P 500
item.description: Stocks tumbled on Wall Street Tuesday as a trade war between the U.S. and its key trading partners escalated and threatened to wipe out all the gains for the S&P 500 since Election Day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How to watch Trump address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to address a joint session of Congress for the first time during his second term on Tuesday, outlining the goals of his administration and how he views the state of the country.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
